pairs.to: Ihya Ulum al-Din (The Revival of the Religious Sciences)

items: Knowledge that is not acted upon becomes evidence against you
Sincerity of intention is the measure of every deed
The nearness of death and the shortness of the road
Counsel is easy; acting on it is the hard and bitter part

tone_register.notes: Warm, dignified, flowing — the Stripe/MIT Press register (house-voice SN-1). A letter from a teacher to a beloved student; second-person address is native to this source.

items: esoteric interpretation (use the term of art if the source gives one)
Quran and hadith quotations preserved verbatim
The Junayd dream (only the night prostrations helped)
The closing supplication, preserved in full
Any terminus technicus glossed once on first use, then kept (SN-7)

audience_calibration.notes: Intelligent general reader; nothing dumbed down, no insider vocabulary assumed (house-voice SN-1).
